§6Chicago Pile§f

§aCrude, early breeding reactor that transmutes uranium into plutonium — no power required!§f

The §bChicago Pile§f is a simple, low-tech breeding reactor made from drilled graphite blocks. It transmutes uranium rods into plutonium without requiring any electricity, making it an excellent early plutonium production method.

§6Construction§f:
§b• Place Graphite Blocks§f in your desired arrangement
§b• Use a Hand Drill§f to drill holes into the graphite
§b• Insert Rods§f into the holes

§6Rod Types§f:
§b• Uranium Rod§f — fissile but not self-sustaining, transmutes to plutonium
§b• Plutonium Rod§f — neutron source to initiate/sustain reactions
§b• Radium Rod§f — neutron source, same as plutonium
§b• Lithium Rod§f — fertile material, transmutes into tritium
§b• Control Rod§f — set downwards to block neutron flux
§b• Control & Detector Rod§f — beeps on neutron detection, can block flux

§6Crafting Rods§f (Tier 2 Anvil):
§b• Uranium Rod§f — 3× Uranium Billet + 2× Iron Plate
§b• Ra226Be Neutron Source§f — 3× Ra226Be Billet + 2× Iron Plate

§6Neutron Mechanics§f:
• Neutrons spread in §erays§f in every direction from source rods
• Uranium rods react and cast their own rays
• Optimal efficiency at §e1.5 blocks§f distance
• Maximum ray range: §e5 blocks§f
• Heat reduces reaction efficiency up to §e50%§f at max
• Solid blocks can block rays (useful for isolating multiple piles)

§6Heat Management§f (max 1,000 before explosion):
• Natural cooling: §e5%§f current heat per tick
• Aluminum Shell: adds §e1.5%§f cooling
• Fans: §e2.5%§f per fan per tick
• Max with shell + 2 fans: §e11.5%§f per tick

§6Depletion§f: After §e50,000§f flux (75,000 in 528 mode), uranium rod transmutes into a plutonium rod (neutron source, 3 flux per ray).

§6Processing§f: Bred plutonium rods can be processed in a §eTier 2 Anvil§f into Reactor-Grade Plutonium for use in ZIRNOX and RBMK reactors.

§eDesign Tip§f: Use thin sheets of graphite blocks with one neutron source — the 5×5 design is a widely favored layout. Multiple isolated reactors allow more efficient setups.
